items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce | APSB26-73
items.summary: This bulletin addresses multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities, including arbitrary code execution, unrestricted file upload, and stored cross-site scripting.

items.title: Improper output encoding in webhooks leading to arbitrary code execution
items.summary: An unauthenticated, zero-interaction flaw in the webhooks component of Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source can lead to arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
items.summary: This vulnerability allows an attacker to upload files with dangerous types, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source.

items.title: Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A stored cross-site scripting flaw enables privilege escalation by injecting scripts into the admin backend, which executes in the browser of an administrator.

items.title: Amasty patches 25 Magento extensions, 1 critical
items.summary: Amasty released a security update for 25 Magento extensions, with one critical vulnerability that could lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, enabling remote code execution.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce - APSB25-50
items.summary: This update resolves critical and important vulnerabilities, including a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability via server-side template injection (CVE-2025-47110).

items.title: Stored XSS via Server-Side Template Injection in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability via server-side template injection affects multiple versions of Adobe Commerce.

items.title: Adobe Commerce DOS Vulnerability
items.summary: A denial-of-service vulnerability in Adobe Commerce caused by a dependency on a vulnerable third-party component, which can be triggered remotely without authentication.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A critical flaw in Magento's REST API allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store by disguising code as an image.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source (APSB26-05)
items.summary: This critical security update addresses multiple vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, and execute unauthorized malicious code.
